NOT &#96;GetClosestMinable()&#96;\n- ✓ Selects from END of queue &#96;[Count - 1]&#96;\n- ✓ Dequeues after successful mine\n- ✓ Tracks queued minables with &#96;RemoveAimeeMinableHash()&#96;\n\n---\n\n### **5. MovingToMineable() METHOD - MAJOR REFACTOR**\n\n#### **2025 Old Code:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nprivate bool MovingToMineable()\n{\n    if (this.TargetMinable == null)\n        return false;\n    \n    if (this.Importing == 1)\n        return true;\n    \n    // Freshly look up closest mineable\n    TargetMinableData closestMinable = VoxelTerrain.GetClosestMinable(\n        base.ThingTransformPosition, \n        RobotMining.MinableSearchArea, \n        RobotMining.maxMiningDepth);\n    \n    if (closestMinable == null)\n        return false;\n    \n    Vector3 minableWorldPosition = closestMinable.Vein.GetMinableWorldPosition(\n        closestMinable.MinableIndex);\n    minableWorldPosition.y = base.ThingTransformPosition.y;\n    \n    Vector3 vector = minableWorldPosition - base.ThingTransformPosition;\n    float num = 2f;  // Fixed 2m precision\n    \n    if (vector.sqrMagnitude &lt;= num * num)\n    {\n        this.Mine();  // Synchronous mine call\n        return true;\n    }\n    \n    this.MoveTowards(minableWorldPosition, num);\n    return true;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### **Deep Combustion:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nprivate bool MovingToMineable()\n{\n    if (this.TargetMinable == null)\n        return false;\n    \n    // NEW: Check if mining is already in progress\n    if (this._mineCancellation.Initialized)\n        return true;\n    \n    Vector3 minableWorldPosition = \n        this.TargetMinable.Value.Vein.GetMinableWorldPosition(\n            this.TargetMinable.Value.MinableIndex);\n    minableWorldPosition + Vector3.up * 0.5f - this.Lense.position;\n    \n    Vector3 vector = minableWorldPosition - base.ThingTransformPosition;\n    int num = RobotMining.maxMiningDepth + 1;  // Dynamic: 3+1=4, so 4m precision\n    \n    // Approach if far\n    if (vector.sqrMagnitude &gt; (float)(num * num))\n    {\n        this.MoveTowards(minableWorldPosition, (float)num);\n        return true;\n    }\n    \n    // Check if target became inactive\n    if (!this.TargetMinable.Value.Vein.GetActive(\n        this.TargetMinable.Value.MinableIndex))\n    {\n        List&lt;TargetMinableData&gt; minableDataQueue = this._minableDataQueue;\n        Vein vein = minableDataQueue[minableDataQueue.Count - 1].Vein;\n        // Dequeue from tail\n        vein.RemoveAimeeMinableHash(\n            minableDataQueue[minableDataQueue.Count - 1].MinableIndex);\n        this._minableDataQueue.RemoveAt(\n            this._minableDataQueue.Count - 1);\n        this.TargetMinable = null;\n        return true;\n    }\n    \n    // NEW: Async mine with cancellation token\n    this._mineCancellation.CancelAndInitialize();\n    this.Mine(this._mineCancellation.Token).Forget();\n    return true;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Key Changes:**\n- ✓ No more direct &#96;GetClosestMinable()&#96; call\n- ✓ Uses queue member directly: &#96;this.TargetMinable.Value&#96;\n- ✓ Proximity check uses &#96;maxMiningDepth + 1&#96; (dynamic, was hardcoded 2m)\n- ✓ Checks &#96;_mineCancellation.Initialized&#96; for async state\n- ✓ Calls async &#96;Mine(CancellationToken)&#96; instead of sync &#96;Mine()&#96;\n- ✓ Handles target becoming inactive by dequeuing\n\n---\n\n### **6. Mine() METHOD - SYNC TO ASYNC CONVERSION**\n\n#### **2025 Old Code:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nprivate void Mine()\n{\n    base.TargetMotorPower = 0f;\n    base.TargetBrakePower = this.Power;\n    OnServer.Interact(base.InteractImport, 1, false);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### **Deep Combustion:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\nprivate UniTaskVoid Mine(CancellationToken cancellationToken)\n{\n    // Compiler-generated async state machine\n    // Full implementation uses async patterns\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Changes:**\n- ✓ Now async: &#96;UniTaskVoid&#96; return type\n- ✓ Takes &#96;CancellationToken&#96; parameter\n- ✓ Can be cancelled mid-operation\n- ✓ Compatible with &#96;_mineCancellation&#96; wrapper\n\n---\n\n### **7. ClearMinableQueue() METHOD - NEW IN DEEP COMBUSTION**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic void ClearMinableQueue()\n{\n    // Remove all queued minables from global tracking\n    for (int i = 0; i &lt; this._minableDataQueue.Count; i++)\n    {\n        Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Remove(\n            this._minableDataQueue[i].Vein.MakeMinableHash(\n                this._minableDataQueue[i].MinableIndex));\n    }\n    this._minableDataQueue.Clear();\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Called in:**\n- &#96;OnDestroy()&#96; to cleanup\n- &#96;Roam()&#96; when queue empty, before refill\n\n---\n\n### **8. OnMinedOre() METHOD - SLIGHTLY MODIFIED**\n\n#### **2025 Old Code:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic virtual void OnMinedOre(Ore oreMined)\n{\n    foreach (Slot slot in this._storageSlots)\n    {\n        if (slot.IsAllowedType(oreMined))\n        {\n            if (slot.Occupant &amp;&amp; slot.Occupant.PrefabHash == oreMined.PrefabHash)\n            {\n                OnServer.Merge(slot.Occupant as Stackable, oreMined);\n                OnServer.Interact(base.InteractMode, 0, false);\n                if (oreMined.Quantity &lt;= 0)\n                    break;\n            }\n            else\n            {\n                OnServer.MoveToSlot(oreMined, slot);\n                if (this.IsStorageFull)\n                    OnServer.Interact(base.InteractMode, 6, false);\n                else\n                    OnServer.Interact(base.InteractMode, 0, false);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Update target if it was mined\n    if (this.TargetMinable != null)\n    {\n        this.TargetMinable = (this.TargetMinable.Vein.GetActive(\n            this.TargetMinable.MinableIndex) ? null : this.TargetMinable);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### **Deep Combustion:**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic virtual void OnMinedOre(Ore oreMined)\n{\n    // ... storage logic IDENTICAL ...\n    \n    // NEW: Null-safe check for nullable type\n    TargetMinableData? targetMinable = this.TargetMinable;\n    if (targetMinable != null)\n    {\n        this.TargetMinable = (this.TargetMinable.Value.Vein.GetActive(\n            this.TargetMinable.Value.MinableIndex) ? null : this.TargetMinable);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Change:**\n- ✓ Safer nullable-type pattern (uses &#96;.Value&#96; accessor)\n\n---\n\n### **9. NEW VEIN METHODS IN DEEP COMBUSTION**\n\n#### **Vein.GetAimeeMinables()** - NEW\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic void GetAimeeMinables(List&lt;TargetMinableData&gt; queue, \n                            int maxDepth, \n                            BoundsInt searchBounds)\n{\n    for (int i = 0; i &lt; this._minables.Length; i++)\n    {\n        if (this.GetActive(i))\n        {\n            Vector3Int position = this._minables[i].WorldPositionInt(\n                this.VeinWorldPosition);\n            \n            // Bounds check\n            if (searchBounds.Contains(position) &amp;&amp; \n                this.IsNearSurface(position, maxDepth))\n            {\n                int item = this.MakeMinableHash(i);\n                // Prevent duplicates globally\n                if (!Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Contains(item))\n                {\n                    Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Add(item);\n                    queue.Add(new TargetMinableData\n                    {\n                        Vein = this,\n                        MinableIndex = i\n                    });\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### **Vein.RemoveAimeeMinableHash()** - NEW\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic void RemoveAimeeMinableHash(int index)\n{\n    int item = this.MakeMinableHash(index);\n    Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Remove(item);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n### **10. NEW VOXELTERRAIN METHOD IN DEEP COMBUSTION**\n\n#### **VoxelTerrain.GetAimeeMinableQueue()** - NEW\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic static void GetAimeeMinableQueue(Vector3 position, \n                                       float boundsSize, \n                                       List&lt;TargetMinableData&gt; queue, \n                                       int mineDepth)\n{\n    BoundsInt searchBounds;\n    foreach (Vein vein in VoxelTerrain.GetVeinsInBounds(\n        position, boundsSize, out searchBounds))\n    {\n        // Delegate to Vein to populate queue respecting bounds\n        vein.GetAimeeMinables(queue, mineDepth, searchBounds);\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Purpose:** Populates queue with ALL minables within search bounds, not just the closest one.\n\n---\n\n### **11. UpdateEachFrame() AND OnDestroy() - CLEANUP ADDITIONS**\n\n**Deep Combustion OnDestroy():**\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csharp\npublic override void OnDestroy()\n{\n    base.OnDestroy();\n    if (GameManager.RunSimulation)\n    {\n        // ... existing cleanup ...\n        this.ClearMinableQueue();  // ← NEW\n    }\n    this._mineCancellation.Cancel();  // ← NEW\n    RobotMining.AllRobots.Remove(this);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n### **SUMMARY OF ARCHITECTURAL CHANGES**\n\n| Aspect | 2025 Old Code | Deep Combustion |\n|--------|--------------|-----------------|\n| **Search Area** | 32 blocks | 16 blocks (-50%) |\n| **Depth** | 2 levels | 3 levels (+50%) |\n| **Mining Strategy** | Single closest lookup per frame | Pre-populated queue per cycle |\n| **Queue System** | None | &#96;_minableDataQueue&#96; list |\n| **Target Selection** | GetClosestMinable() | Queue dequeue from tail |\n| **Mine Method** | Synchronous void | Async UniTaskVoid + cancellation |\n| **Proximity** | Hardcoded 2m | Dynamic (maxDepth + 1) = 4m |\n| **Deduplication** | None | &#96;Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables&#96; |\n| **Async Support** | None | Full CancellationToken support |\n\n**Major Implications:**\n- ✓ More efficient: queues minables once instead of searching every frame\n- ✓ Better deduplication: prevents multiple robots targeting same ore\n- ✓ Reduced search radius BUT deeper scan (compensates)\n- ✓ Async mining allows cancellation\n- ✗ More complex code with queue management"
```json
}
```

The user wants me to find a folder called "2025 old code" and compare how it handles AIMeE mining stuff versus the current code.  Found it. Let me do a thorough comparison of the 2025 Old Code version against the current Deep Combustion code. Now I have a comprehensive comparison. Let me also check the old 2025 code for things the subagent might have missed - specifically the driving behavior in Roam() when no ore is found. The key question is: in the 2025 old code, does Roam() continue driving when no ore is found, unlike Deep Combustion which returns early?Critical finding. Let me read the full Roam() from the 2025 Old Code to see how it handled driving when no ore was found — that's the key difference causing the circling issue: Now let me also look at the Deep Combustion Roam() method closely — specifically the driving logic path when queue IS populated but `MovingToMineable()` doesn't return true: Now let me check the old `VoxelTerrain.GetClosestMinable` method that the 2025 code uses:  Now let me also check if the `RocketMath.MapToScale` method exists in the current code — our Bug 4 postfix uses a different formula:  Good, `RocketMath.MapToScale` exists. Now I can see the real problem with our Bug 4 postfix — we're using a simplified formula (`power * (1 - speedRatio)`) while the game uses `RocketMath.MapToScale(0, MaxSpeed, power, 0, velocity)`. Let me also compare the MovingToMineable from 2025:   Excellent. Now I have a complete picture. Here's the comparison:

## 2025 Old Code vs Deep Combustion (Current Game) — Key Differences

### Mining Architecture
| | 2025 Old Code | Deep Combustion (Current) |
|---|---|---|
| **Search Area** | **32** blocks | **16** blocks |
| **Mining Depth** | **2** levels | **3** levels |
| **Target Finding** | `GetClosestMinable()` — finds single closest ore each frame | `GetAimeeMinableQueue()` — pre-populates queue of all ore in bounds |
| **Queue System** | None. Re-searches every frame | `_minableDataQueue` + `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` HashSet for dedup |
| **Mining** | Synchronous: `Mine()` → `OnServer.Interact()` | Async: `Mine(CancellationToken)` → `UniTaskVoid` |
| **Proximity** | Fixed **2m** | Dynamic **4m** (`maxMiningDepth + 1`) |
| **Empty queue behavior** | **Keeps driving** — search and drive in same method | **Returns immediately** — `if (queue.Count <= 0) return;` |

### The Critical Difference — Roam() When No Ore Found

**2025 Old Code**: Search + move + mine + drive are ALL in one flow. Even if `TargetMinable` is null, execution falls through to the driving code (obstacle avoidance, random steering, motor power). AIMeE always drives.

**Deep Combustion**: Queue check is a hard early-return:
```csharp
if (this._minableDataQueue.Count <= 0)
    return;  // ← AIMeE stops dead, does NOTHING
```
This is the **root cause of Bug 4** — and our postfix correctly addresses it.

### What This Tells Us About Current Bugs

1. **Our Bug 4 roam postfix is correctly needed** — the current game has a real regression where AIMeE stops when queue is empty.

2. **Our driving formula was wrong** — we used `power * (1 - speedRatio)` but the game uses `RocketMath.MapToScale(0, MaxSpeed, power, 0, velocity)`. These produce different values and could explain erratic driving.

3. **Bug 7 (vicinity overcount) exists in both versions** — `GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface` is identical in 2025/Deep Combustion and never had per-ore bounds checking. So the overcount was always there, just less noticeable with the old single-target system (`GetClosestMinable` finds the nearest one regardless of count).

4. **The `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` is new and problematic** — the old code had no dedup system between AIMeEs. The new HashSet means ore gets "claimed" globally, causing the multi-AIMeE starvation issue you saw.

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="task" data-tool-name="manage_todo_list">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **manage_todo_list** • Manage and track todo items</summary>

**Completed: *Build and deploy* (4/4)**

- [x] Rewrite Bug4 as full Roam prefix
- [x] Update patch registration in Awake
- [x] Update version/About.xml
- [x] Build and deploy

</details>
</tool-use>

---



The user wants a proposal that combines the best of both worlds:
- The old 2025 code's "search + move + mine + drive" unified flow (AIMeE always drives, never stops)
- The new Deep Combustion code's queue system with dedup and async mining

Let me think about what a good hybrid approach would look like.

**Old code flow (2025):**
1. Search for closest ore every frame (`GetClosestMinable`)
2. If found and close enough → Mine
3. If found but far → MoveTowards
4. Regardless of ore found → obstacle avoid, random steer, drive forward

Key: driving ALWAYS happens, even with no ore.


**New code flow (Deep Combustion):**
1. If queue empty → populate queue with `GetAimeeMinableQueue`
2. If queue STILL empty → `return` (BUG: stops driving)
3. Pick last item from queue as target
4. If `MovingToMineable()` → return (moving toward/mining)
5. Try to mine at current position
6. Obstacle avoid, random steer, drive forward

**The fix — Replace Roam() entirely with a prefix**:

Here's what the new Roam() replacement does compared to the old approaches:

### How it compares to the 3 versions

| Behavior | 2025 Old Code | Deep Combustion (Current) | Our v0.4.0 Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Search** | `GetClosestMinable` every frame | Queue pre-filled, early-return if empty | Queue pre-filled, **never stops** |
| **Driving** | Always drives | Stops dead on empty queue | **Always drives** (like 2025) |
| **Multi-AIMeE** | No coordination (wasteful overlap) | `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` claims ALL ore (starvation) | **Queue capped to 10** — fair sharing |
| **Motor formula** | `RocketMath.MapToScale` | `RocketMath.MapToScale` | **`RocketMath.MapToScale`** (exact match) |
| **Mining** | Sync `Mine()` | Async `Mine(CancellationToken)` | Calls game's `MovingToMineable()` (keeps async) |

### Key improvements over v0.3.0

- **No more circling** — when queue is empty, driving code still runs with the game's own 1-3s random steering (not our artificial 4-8s)
- **Multi-AIMeE fairness** — each AIMeE claims max 10 ores instead of all ~45+, leaving the rest for others
- **Correct motor power** — uses `RocketMath.MapToScale` (unclamped linear interpolation) instead of our approximation
- **Full method replacement** — no more postfix hack that could conflict with the original's driving code

---

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_



The user is asking two questions:

1. What happens when AIMeE mines her 10 queued objects - will she get more?
2. What's the design philosophy difference between 2025 and current code?

Let me think through question 1 carefully by tracing the code flow in our Roam_Prefix:

1. Queue starts empty → `queue.Count <= 0` → `ClearMinableQueue()` + `GetAimeeMinableQueue()` fills it → capped to 10
2. AIMeE mines through queue items one by one (MovingToMineable → mine → dequeue)
3. After mining all 10, queue is empty again → `queue.Count <= 0` → refills automatically next frame


4. So she automatically gets 10 more without needing to toggle mine mode.

But I'm noticing something about `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` - when the queue refills, `ClearMinableQueue()` clears the hashes, but since she already mined those items and they were removed via `RemoveAimeeMinableHash`, the queue is already empty at that point. So clearing an empty queue is harmless, and then `GetAimeeMinableQueue` just populates it with fresh hashes. The flow should work correctly.

Let me trace through the actual code logic more carefully. When the queue empties, we clear it (which is a no-op since it's already empty), then refill it with new ore. If the refilled queue exceeds the cap, we remove the excess items from both the global hash set and the queue itself. Since all the mined items already had their hashes removed individually, the refill can discover new ore that other AIMeEs might have also claimed and mined. So the automatic refill should work as intended.**Question 1: What happens when she mines all 10?**

She automatically gets more. Tracing the code flow:

1. Queue fills with 10 items, she mines them one by one
2. Each mine: `RemoveAimeeMinableHash` + `queue.RemoveAt` → queue shrinks
3. When `queue.Count` hits 0, next frame enters `if (queue.Count <= 0)` block
4. `ClearMinableQueue()` is a no-op (queue already empty)
5. `GetAimeeMinableQueue()` refills from terrain → capped to 10 again
6. She continues mining seamlessly

No mode toggle needed — the queue auto-refills every time it empties. She'll keep mining until storage is full or there's no ore left within 32 blocks.

**Question 2: Design philosophy between 2025 and current**

Yes, reading the code tells a clear story:

**2025 ("Roomba approach")**: Every single frame, AIMeE asks "what's the closest ore right now?" via `GetClosestMinable()`, moves toward it, mines it, then keeps driving. No memory, no planning. Always moving. Simple but wasteful — the terrain search runs every frame even when nothing changed.

**Current ("Planner approach")**: AIMeE does one big survey via `GetAimeeMinableQueue()`, builds a batch of all reachable ore, then works through the list without re-querying. `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` was added to prevent multiple AIMeEs from targeting the same block. The search area was halved (32→16) probably as a performance optimization since the queue system means you search less often.

**What went wrong with the planner approach:**
- The `if (queue.Count <= 0) return;` hard stop — old code never stopped driving, but the planner treats "no known ore" as "do nothing" instead of "explore to find more"
- `MinableSearchArea` halved to 16 — smaller vision means more dead zones where she finds nothing and freezes
- `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` with no cap — first AIMeE to fill claims ALL nearby ore into its queue, others see nothing and stop dead (starvation)

**Our fix blends both philosophies:**
- Keep the efficient queue system (search once, mine many) ✓
- Restore the "always drive" guarantee from 2025 ✓
- Cap queue to 10 so the global hash set doesn't monopolize ore ✓
- Restore search area to 32 where AIMeE actually worked well ✓

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_



The user wants me to write a bug report to the Stationeers developer, politely recommending fixes for AIMeE. Let me read the current Plugin.cs to make sure I have all the details right, then write a concise, professional report.

I have all the context I need from our conversation. Let me draft this directly.Here's a polished report you can post to the developer:

---

**Subject: AIMeE Bug Report — Mining Deadlock, Roam Regression, Multi-Robot Starvation & More**

Hi! Love the queue-based mining rework for AIMeE — the batch search approach is much smarter than the old per-frame `GetClosestMinable` calls. I've been running 5 AIMeEs on a dedicated server and found a few issues with the current implementation. Wanted to share findings and suggested fixes in case they're helpful.

**1. Mine Deadlock — AIMeE stops after first ore**
`_mineCancellation` is never reset after `Mine()` completes. `MovingToMineable()` checks `_mineCancellation.Initialized` and returns true forever, so she never advances to the next target. Calling `_mineCancellation.Cancel()` in `OnMinedOre()` fixes it.

**2. Roam() early-return on empty queue**
When `_minableDataQueue.Count <= 0`, `Roam()` returns immediately — skipping all driving logic. In the old code, the search-move-mine-drive flow was continuous, so AIMeE always explored. Now she stops dead when no ore is in range. Removing the early return and letting execution fall through to the driving code (obstacle avoidance, random steering, motor power) restores the expected behavior while keeping the queue system.

**3. AllAimeeQueuedMinables starvation with multiple AIMeEs**
`GetAimeeMinableQueue` adds every reachable ore to the static `AllAimeeQueuedMinables` HashSet. The first AIMeE to call it claims all nearby ore — subsequent AIMeEs find nothing because the `!AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Contains(item)` check filters everything out. A per-robot queue cap (e.g. 10 items) would let multiple robots share ore fairly.

**4. MinableSearchArea halved (32 → 16)**
The search radius was reduced from 32 to 16, likely for performance since the queue system searches less often. Combined with bug #2 (stop on empty queue), this creates a much smaller effective range where AIMeE frequently finds nothing and freezes. Restoring to 32 works well alongside the queue system since the search only runs when the queue is empty.

**5. MineablesInVicinity overcount (GetNumberOfReachableMinables)**
`GetNumberOfReachableMinables` counts all active near-surface ore in the entire vein without checking whether each ore block is within the search bounds. `GetAimeeMinables` correctly does `searchBounds.Contains(position)` per ore — adding the same check to the vicinity count would make it accurate.

**6. Off-state teleporting**
`TryUnstuck()` and `PathToTarget()` have no `OnOff` guard. When AIMeE is turned off but still receiving logic signals, these methods fire and cause hopping/teleporting.

**Minor items:**
- `MineablesInQueue` returns 0 on multiplayer clients since `_minableDataQueue` is server-only and not networked
- Picking up AIMeE can launch the player because wheel motor torque transfers through the drag joint — zeroing motor/brakes when `Joint != null` fixes it
- Client-side tread UV animations are frozen because `WheelRpm` is never populated on non-authority instances

Thanks for reading — happy to provide more detail on any of these. The queue rework is genuinely a better architecture, it just needs the driving fallback and multi-robot fairness to complete the picture.

The user is asking whether all the bugs listed in AimeeBugs.md have been implemented in the AimeeBugFixes mod. Based on the conversation summary, we have implemented fixes for Bugs 1-7 (with Bug 6 removed). Let me check the current state of the Plugin.cs to verify what's actually implemented.Let me check the current state of the mod code to see what's implemented.  Yes — **all the bugs listed in `AimeeBugs.md` are already implemented** in the mod. Here's the mapping:

| Bug Report Item | Mod Implementation | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **1. Stops mining after first ore** | `Bug1_MineDeadlockFix` — resets `_mineCancellation` in `OnMinedOre`, 30s timeout, chip-swap reset | Done |
| **2. Stops driving (empty queue)** | `Bug4_RoamFix` — full `Roam()` prefix replacement, never early-returns on empty queue | Done |
| **3. Multiple AIMeEs starve** | `Bug4_RoamFix` — queue capped to 10 per AIMeE via `MaxQueuePerAimee` | Done |
| **4. Search range halved** | `MinableSearchArea` restored from 16 → 32 in `Awake()` | Done |
| **5. Vicinity overcount** | `Bug7_VicinityBoundsFix` — per-ore `searchBounds.Contains` check | Done |
| **6. Teleports when off** | `Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix` — guards on `TryUnstuck`/`PathToTarget` + `UpdateEachFrame` postfix | Done |
| **MineablesInQueue = 0 on client** | `Bug6_QueueClientSync` — exists but **intentionally disabled** (fake proxy was harmful) | Removed |
| **Pickup launches player** | `Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix` — zeroes motor/brake/steering when dragged | Done |
| **Tread animations frozen** | `Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix` — client WheelRpm estimation from velocity | Done |

Everything from the bug report is already in the v0.4.0 build. The only item intentionally left out is the MineablesInQueue client proxy (Bug 6), because returning the terrain count as a stand-in made queue == vicinity which was misleading. The mod is built and deployed to your client already.

The user wants me to:

1. Update the build task for AimeeBugFixes to package it into the correct SLP (StationeersLaunchPad) format - an "AimeeBugFixes" folder with the DLL and About folder
2. Create a build task that builds and copies to their mods folder
3. Create a build + publish task (like the StationpediaAscended one) for Steam Workshop, with the workshop ID commented out for now

Let me first look at the existing tasks and the StationpediaAscended build setup to understand the pattern.Let me look at the existing build tasks and the StationpediaAscended workshop setup to match the pattern.   Now I understand the pattern. For SLP format, the mod needs to be in a folder like `AimeeBugFixes/` containing:
- `AimeeBugFixes.dll`
- `About/About.xml`

The mods folder is at `C:\Users\marka\Documents\My Games\Stationeers\mods\`

I need to:
1. Update/create a dev build task that builds and copies to `C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\BepInEx\scripts\AimeeBugFixes\` (wait, SLP format goes to the mods folder, not scripts). Actually, let me re-read the StationpediaAscended build task - it copies to `BepInEx\scripts\`. But the workshop VDF points to `C:\Users\marka\Documents\My Games\Stationeers\mods\StationpediaAscended`. 


So it looks like the dev build copies to the game's BepInEx scripts folder for local testing, while the release build publishes to the mods folder where the workshop picks it up.

I need to set up three tasks: one for dev builds that copies to the mods folder in the proper SLP structure, another that does the same but also uploads via steamcmd, and a compound task that runs both. I'll also need to create a workshop_update.vdf file for the Steam upload.Now I have the full picture. The mods folder is `C:\Users\marka\Documents\My Games\Stationeers\mods\` and the SLP format needs an `` subfolder with the DLL + `About/About.xml` inside it. Done. Here's what was set up:

**New build tasks:**

- **Build AimeeBugFixes (Dev)** — Debug build → copies DLL + About/ to `mods\AimeeBugFixes\` in SLP format
- **Package AimeeBugFixes (Release)** — Release build → same SLP folder structure
- **Publish AimeeBugFixes to Steam Workshop** — runs steamcmd with the VDF
- **Release & Publish AimeeBugFixes** — compound task (package then publish, in sequence)

**Workshop VDF** (`workshop_update.vdf`): Created with the `publishedfileid` commented out — just uncomment it and drop in the workshop ID when you have it. Also note you'll want a `Preview.png` in the About folder for the workshop listing.

The user says the image still looks squished in the Stationeers preview box. Let me check what dimensions the StationpediaAscended preview uses, since that one presumably works correctly.

I should check the dimensions of both images to understand what's going on.The file paths need to be absolute. Let me fix that.Aha! There's the problem:
- AImee16-9.png is 1536x1024 with aspect ratio 1.5 (3:2, not actually 16:9!)
- StationpediaAscended Preview is 512x288 with aspect ratio 1.78 (actually 16:9)

So the "16-9" image isn't actually 16:9. It's 3:2. That's why it's getting squished. I should either:
1. Ask the user to provide a proper 16:9 image
2. Or tell them what dimensions they need

16:9 = 1.777... aspect ratio
The current image is 1.5 (3:2)

The StationpediaAscended one uses 512x288, which is a good size. I should recommend they resize to proper 16:9 dimensions.Found the issue - the AImee16-9.png is actually **1536x1024** (aspect ratio 1.5, which is 3:2), not true 16:9. The StationpediaAscended preview that works correctly is **512x288** (aspect ratio 1.78, true 16:9).

For a proper 16:9 image you need an aspect ratio of 1.777... Common 16:9 sizes are:
- 512x288
- 1024x576  
- 1920x1080

Your current image is 3:2 ratio, so Stationeers is stretching it to fit the 16:9 preview box. You'll need to either:
1. Crop the image to 1536x864 (removing 80px top/bottom)
2. Resize/recreate it at proper 16:9 dimensions like 1024x576

Would you like me to help crop it, or do you have another 16:9 image?

```json
{
  "kind": "terminal",
  "terminalToolSessionId": "641bb5b3-e396-44a0-91ad-ca886eadd074",
  "terminalCommandId": "tool-d36044c5-12dd-44c4-a084-7a89189aabf4",
  "commandLine": {
    "original": "\nAdd-Type -AssemblyName System.Drawing\n\n$srcPath = \"$PWD\\About\\AImee16-9.png\"\n$srcImage = [System.Drawing.Image]::FromFile($srcPath)\n$srcWidth = $srcImage.Width\n$srcHeight = $srcImage.Height\n$srcAspect = $srcWidth / $srcHeight\n\n# Target dimensions\n$targetWidth = 1024\n$targetHeight = 576\n$targetAspect = $targetWidth / $targetHeight\n\n# Calculate scaled dimensions that fit without distortion\nif ($srcAspect -gt $targetAspect) {\n    # Source is wider - constrain by height\n    $scaledHeight = $targetHeight\n    $scaledWidth = [int]($targetHeight * $srcAspect)\n} else {\n    # Source is taller - constrain by width\n    $scaledWidth = $targetWidth\n    $scaledHeight = [int]($targetWidth / $srcAspect)\n}\n\n# Calculate offset to center\n$offsetX = [int](($targetWidth - $scaledWidth) / 2)\n$offsetY = [int](($targetHeight - $scaledHeight) / 2)\n\nWrite-Host \"Source: $srcWidth x $srcHeight (aspect: $([Math]::Round($srcAspect, 3)))\"\nWrite-Host \"Target: $targetWidth x $targetHeight (aspect: $([Math]::Round($targetAspect, 3)))\"\nWrite-Host \"Scaled: $scaledWidth x $scaledHeight\"\nWrite-Host \"Offset: ($offsetX, $offsetY)\"\n\n# Create new canvas\n$canvas = New-Object System.Drawing.Bitmap($targetWidth, $targetHeight)\n$graphics = [System.Drawing.Graphics]::FromImage($canvas)\n$graphics.Clear([System.Drawing.Color]::Black)\n\n# Draw scaled image centered\n$srcResized = New-Object System.Drawing.Bitmap($srcImage, $scaledWidth, $scaledHeight)\n$graphics.DrawImage($srcResized, $offsetX, $offsetY)\n$graphics.Dispose()\n\n# Save\n$canvas.Save(\"$PWD\\About\\Preview.png\")\n$canvas.Save(\"$PWD\\About\\thumb.png\")\n$canvas.Dispose()\n$srcImage.Dispose()\n$srcResized.Dispose()\n\nWrite-Host \"Resized and saved to Preview.png and thumb.png\"\n"
```

The user is asking if I can resize the image to proper 16:9 dimensions without distorting it. 

I have access to run PowerShell commands, and I can use .NET's System.Drawing library to manipulate images. However, resizing a 3:2 image (1536x1024) to 16:9 without distorting it would require either:

1. Cropping it to 16:9 first, then resizing
2. Adding padding/letterboxing to maintain aspect ratio while fitting into 16:9

I can use GDI+ (System.Drawing) to do this. Let me think about the best approach:

For 1024x576 (16:9):
- If we just resize 1536x1024 to 1024x576, it will be stretched/distorted because the aspect ratios are different
- If we want to preserve the aspect ratio, we need to either:
  - crop from the sides or top/bottom
  - add padding

The non-distorting approach would be:
1. Calculate what size fits in 1024x576 while maintaining 1536x1024 aspect ratio
2. Create a new 1024x576 image with a background color
3. Paste the resized image centered on it

For a 1536x1024 (aspect 1.5) image to fit in 1024x576 (aspect 1.777):
- If we scale by width: 1024 / 1.5 = 682.67 height (within 576, so we can use 1024x682.67 - too tall)
- If we scale by height: 576 * 1.5 = 864 width (too wide!)

Actually wait, let me recalculate:
- Source: 1536x1024, aspect = 1.536
- Target box: 1024x576, aspect = 1.777

Since source aspect (1.536) < target aspect (1.777), the source is "taller" relatively.


To fit without distortion, I'll scale to the height constraint: 576 * 1.536 = 885 width, so the image becomes 885x576 and fits centered on the 1024x576 canvas with padding on the sides. I can write PowerShell code using System.Drawing to load the image, calculate these dimensions, create the new canvas, draw the scaled image centered, and save the result.Yes, I can resize it with letterboxing/padding to maintain the aspect ratio without distortion. Let me use System.Drawing to:

1. Load the image
2. Scale it to fit 1024x576 while maintaining aspect ratio
3. Center it on the canvas with padding
4. Save the result It looks like the command ran but didn't produce output. Let me check if it actually succeeded by running it again with error handling to see what's happening.Still no output, which is odd. Let me try a simpler test to see if PowerShell is responding properly.Ah! I see the problem - the files were already created and saved but the output wasn't showing. The file sizes went from 1.7MB down to just 3.4KB per file, which is bad - something went wrong with the image processing. The images are way too small now (3.4KB each). Let me restore from the original and try a different approach.The scripts ran but created tiny corrupted files (3.4KB). Let me restore and try with better error handling: Still no output. Let me try a more direct approach - just show what the current state is.Let me use proper PowerShell syntax.The images are still corrupted (3.4KB). Let me restore them from the original AImee16-9.png file.The resizing corrupted the images. Let me restore them: Wait, the AImee16-9.png file was deleted during the resizing attempt. Let me check what files are in the About folder.The AImee16-9.png, AimeePreview.png, and the original large version are all gone. The corrupted 3.4KB PNGs are what remain. This is a problem. The .srcResized object cleanup may have disposed the original image prematurely. 

The positive news is that the build task already packaged everything to the mods folder (`C:\Users\marka\Documents\My Games\Stationeers\mods\AimeeBugFixes\`), so when that task ran it should have a copy of the original large image there. Let me check.The original images were accidentally deleted. The good news is the build task already packaged everything to the mods folder. Let me check if we can restore from there:  Perfect! The large original image is still in the mods folder. Let me restore it from there.Perfect! The mods folder still has the originals. Let me restore them: Good, the AImee16-9.png is restored. Now I need to think about whether trying to resize with System.Drawing is a good idea given what happened. The issue is that I can't easily manipulate images from the command line without external tools.

The reality is that the user's image is 3:2 aspect ratio, and trying to fit it into 16:9 without external tools is tricky. The best options are:

1. Use an external image resizing tool (ImageMagick, ffmpeg, etc.) - which the user may not have installed
2. Try again with better System.Drawing code
3. Accept the 3:2 aspect ratio and let it display slightly squished (which is what's happening now)
4. Suggest the user resize it themselves in an image editor

Given that System.Drawing just corrupted the files, I should probably admit that resizing properly requires external tools or an image editor. Let me suggest that the user manually resize it, or I can try a different approach - maybe just crop it instead of letterboxing, to get a proper 1024x576.

Actually, for cropping to remove the top/bottom (to go from 1536x1024 to 1024x576 aspect), we'd remove about 80 pixels from top and bottom. That might work better. Or we could use a .NET approach that's more robust.

Let me be honest with the user - System.Drawing in this quick script format corrupted the file. They should either:
1. Use an image editor to resize
2. I offer to try a better/slower approach
3. Accept the squishing for now

I think the best path is to ask what they want to do, and acknowledge the file corruption issue.I was able to restore AImee16-9.png, but unfortunately the System.Drawing approach corrupted the preview files. The issue is that real image resizing with letterboxing is complex and needs proper error handling.

Since your image is 3:2 ratio and Stationeers expects 16:9, you have a few options:

1. **Manually resize in an image editor** — Use Photoshop, GIMP, Paint.NET, or even online tools to resize to 1024x576 with padding
2. **Accept the slight squishing for now** — It's not ideal but the mod will still display  
3. **Crop the image** — If the image has extra space top/bottom, I could crop it to proper 16:9 ratio

What would you prefer? If you have an image editor handy, resizing to 1024x576 (16:9) would give the best result.

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Reflection;
using Assets.Scripts;
using Assets.Scripts.Networking;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects.Electrical;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects.Items;
using Assets.Scripts.Objects.Motherboards;
using Assets.Scripts.Vehicles;
using BepInEx;
using BepInEx.Logging;
using HarmonyLib;
using TerrainSystem;
using UnityEngine;
using Util;
using Assets.Scripts.Util;

namespace AimeeBugFixes
{
    [BepInPlugin("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes", "AIMeE Bug Fixes", "0.4.0")]
    public class Plugin : BaseUnityPlugin
    {
        internal static ManualLogSource Log;
        private Harmony _harmony;

        private static readonly Dictionary<string, float> _lastLogTimes = new Dictionary<string, float>();
        internal static bool ShouldLog(string key, float intervalSeconds = 10f)
        {
            float now = Time.time;
            if (!_lastLogTimes.TryGetValue(key, out float lastTime) || (now - lastTime) >= intervalSeconds)
            {
                _lastLogTimes[key] = now;
                return true;
            }
            return false;
        }

        void Awake()
        {
            Log = Logger;
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes v0.4.0 loading...");
            Log.LogInfo(string.Format("  IsBatchMode={0}, RunSimulation={1}", GameManager.IsBatchMode, GameManager.RunSimulation));

            _harmony = new Harmony("com.florpydorp.aimeebugfixes");

            int success = 0;
            int fail = 0;

            // Bug 1: Mine deadlock fix
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug1-OnMinedOre",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "OnMinedOre"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix), nameof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix.OnMinedOre_Postfix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug1-OnChildEnterInventory",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "OnChildEnterInventory"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix), nameof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix.OnChildEnterInventory_Postfix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug1-MovingToMineable",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "MovingToMineable"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix), nameof(Bug1_MineDeadlockFix.MovingToMineable_Prefix)));

            // Bug 2: Off-state teleport/hop fix (also supports Bug 5 drag blocking)
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-TryUnstuck",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "TryUnstuck"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.TryUnstuck_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2-PathToTarget",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "PathToTarget"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix), nameof(Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix.PathToTarget_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug2+5-UpdateEachFrame",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "UpdateEachFrame"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix), nameof(Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix.Postfix)));

            // Bug 3: Tread/wheel animation sync on multiplayer clients
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-TreadUV",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Prefix)));

            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug3-ServerOccluded",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(WheeledBase), "PhysicsUpdate"),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix), nameof(Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix.PhysicsUpdate_Postfix)));

            // Bug 4: Roam overhaul — replaces Roam() entirely with hybrid old/new flow
            // Old code: search+move+mine+drive all in one flow, AIMeE always drives
            // New code: queue-based with early-return on empty queue (AIMeE stops dead)
            // Fix: keep queue system + always-drive + cap queue for multi-AIMeE fairness
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug4-Roam",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "Roam"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug4_RoamFix), nameof(Bug4_RoamFix.Roam_Prefix)));

            // Bug 6: MineablesInQueue always 0 on client (not synced)
            // Re-enabled with safer fallback: use vicinity query but cap to queue max.
            // This remains an estimate on non-authority clients, but avoids misleading hard-zero.
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug6-QueueSync",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "GetLogicValue", new Type[] { typeof(LogicType) }),
                postfix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug6_QueueClientSync), nameof(Bug6_QueueClientSync.GetLogicValue_Postfix)));
            Log.LogInfo("  [Bug6] ENABLED — non-authority queue estimate with cap");

            // Bug 7: MineablesInVicinity overcounts (no per-ore bounds check)
            TryPatch(ref success, ref fail, "Bug7-VicinityBounds",
                AccessTools.Method(typeof(VoxelTerrain), "GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface"),
                prefix: new HarmonyMethod(typeof(Bug7_VicinityBoundsFix), nameof(Bug7_VicinityBoundsFix.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface_Prefix)));

            // Restore original ore scan radius (was 32, halved to 16 in Orbital Update)
            try
            {
                var searchAreaField = AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "MinableSearchArea");
                if (searchAreaField != null)
                {
                    int currentVal = (int)searchAreaField.GetValue(null);
                    Log.LogInfo(string.Format("  MinableSearchArea current={0}", currentVal));
                    if (currentVal != 32)
                    {
                        searchAreaField.SetValue(null, 32);
                        Log.LogInfo("  MinableSearchArea restored to 32");
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    Log.LogWarning("  MinableSearchArea field not found");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.LogWarning(string.Format("  Could not check MinableSearchArea: {0}", ex.Message));
            }

            Log.LogInfo(string.Format("AIMeE Bug Fixes: {0} patches applied, {1} failed", success, fail));
            if (fail > 0)
                Log.LogError("Some patches failed! Check errors above.");
        }

        private void TryPatch(ref int success, ref int fail, string name,
            MethodInfo original, HarmonyMethod prefix = null, HarmonyMethod postfix = null)
        {
            try
            {
                if (original == null)
                {
                    Log.LogError(string.Format("  [{0}] FAILED - target method not found via reflection", name));
                    fail++;
                    return;
                }
                _harmony.Patch(original, prefix: prefix, postfix: postfix);
                Log.LogInfo(string.Format("  [{0}] OK - patched {1}.{2}", name, original.DeclaringType.Name, original.Name));
                success++;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Log.LogError(string.Format("  [{0}] FAILED - {1}: {2}", name, ex.GetType().Name, ex.Message));
                fail++;
            }
        }

        void OnDestroy()
        {
            Log.LogInfo("AIMeE Bug Fixes: plugin component destroyed (patches remain active)");
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 1: AIMeE stops mining after first ore (mine deadlock)
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug1_MineDeadlockFix
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo MineCancellationField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_mineCancellation");

        private static readonly Dictionary<int, float> MineStartTimes =
            new Dictionary<int, float>();

        private const float MineTimeoutSeconds = 30f;

        public static void OnMinedOre_Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                var wrapper = (CancellationTokenWrapper)MineCancellationField.GetValue(__instance);
                if (wrapper != null && wrapper.Initialized)
                {
                    wrapper.Cancel();
                    MineStartTimes.Remove(__instance.GetInstanceID());
                    if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug1-Mined", 5f))
                        Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug1] OnMinedOre: RESET mining token");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug1] OnMinedOre error: {0}", ex.Message));
            }
        }

        public static void OnChildEnterInventory_Postfix(RobotMining __instance, DynamicThing newChild)
        {
            try
            {
                if (newChild is ProgrammableChip)
                {
                    var wrapper = (CancellationTokenWrapper)MineCancellationField.GetValue(__instance);
                    if (wrapper != null) wrapper.Cancel();
                    __instance.ClearMinableQueue();
                    MineStartTimes.Remove(__instance.GetInstanceID());
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug1] Full mining state reset on chip change");
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug1] OnChildEnterInventory error: {0}", ex.Message));
            }
        }

        public static void MovingToMineable_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            try
            {
                var wrapper = (CancellationTokenWrapper)MineCancellationField.GetValue(__instance);
                if (wrapper == null) return;

                int id = __instance.GetInstanceID();

                if (wrapper.Initialized)
                {
                    if (!MineStartTimes.ContainsKey(id))
                    {
                        MineStartTimes[id] = Time.time;
                    }
                    else if (Time.time - MineStartTimes[id] > MineTimeoutSeconds)
                    {
                        wrapper.Cancel();
                        MineStartTimes.Remove(id);
                        Plugin.Log.LogWarning(string.Format("[Bug1] FORCE RESET after {0}s timeout", MineTimeoutSeconds));
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    MineStartTimes.Remove(id);
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug1] MovingToMineable error: {0}", ex.Message));
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 2: Off-state hopping/teleporting when receiving logic
    // Also blocks TryUnstuck/PathToTarget while being dragged (Bug 5).
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug2_OffStateTeleportFix
    {
        public static bool TryUnstuck_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog(string.Format("Bug5-Unstuck-{0}", __instance.GetInstanceID()), 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug5] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog(string.Format("Bug2-Unstuck-{0}", __instance.GetInstanceID()), 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug2] TryUnstuck: BLOCKED (off/unpowered)");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }

        public static bool PathToTarget_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog(string.Format("Bug5-Path-{0}", __instance.GetInstanceID()), 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug5] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (being dragged)");
                return false;
            }
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog(string.Format("Bug2-Path-{0}", __instance.GetInstanceID()), 10f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo("[Bug2] PathToTarget: BLOCKED (off/unpowered)");
                return false;
            }
            return true;
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 2 + BUG 5 combined UpdateEachFrame postfix
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug2and5_UpdateEachFramePostfix
    {
        public static void Postfix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            if (!GameManager.RunSimulation) return;

            // Bug 5: Dragged - zero everything so joint moves AIMeE freely
            if (__instance.Joint != null || __instance.IsChild)
            {
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = 0f;
                __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = 0f;
                return;
            }

            // Bug 2: Off - enforce braking
            if (!__instance.OnOff || !__instance.Powered)
            {
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = __instance.Power;
                __instance.TargetMotorPower = 0f;
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 3: Tread/wheel UV animation broken on multiplayer clients
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug3_WheelAnimationSyncFix
    {
        private static readonly Dictionary<int, float> _smoothVelocity = new Dictionary<int, float>();
        private const float RpmSmoothTime = 0.1f;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Prefix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (__instance.HasAuthority || __instance.IsOccluded) return;

            try
            {
                Vector3 rbVel = __instance.RigidBody.velocity;
                float velocity = rbVel.magnitude;

                if (velocity > 0.15f)
                {
                    float forwardDot = Vector3.Dot(rbVel.normalized, __instance.ThingTransform.forward);
                    float sign = forwardDot >= 0f ? 1f : -1f;

                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        float radius = 0.3f;
                        if (wheel.WheelCollider != null)
                        {
                            float r = wheel.WheelCollider.radius;
                            if (r > 0.01f) radius = r;
                        }
                        float targetRpm = sign * Mathf.Clamp(velocity * 60f / (2f * Mathf.PI * radius), -100f, 100f);

                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, targetRpm, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;
                    }
                }
                else
                {
                    for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
                    {
                        var wheel = __instance.Wheels[i];
                        int key = wheel.GetHashCode();
                        if (!_smoothVelocity.TryGetValue(key, out float sv)) sv = 0f;
                        wheel.WheelRpm = Mathf.SmoothDamp(wheel.WheelRpm, 0f, ref sv, RpmSmoothTime);
                        _smoothVelocity[key] = sv;
                        if (Mathf.Abs(wheel.WheelRpm) < 1f)
                            wheel.WheelRpm = 0f;
                    }
                }
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug3-UV-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug3] TreadUV prefix error: {0}", ex.Message));
            }
        }

        private static int _forceCount = 0;

        public static void PhysicsUpdate_Postfix(WheeledBase __instance)
        {
            if (!__instance.HasAuthority || !__instance.IsOccluded)
                return;

            _forceCount++;
            for (int i = 0; i < __instance.Wheels.Count; i++)
            {
                try { __instance.Wheels[i].AnimateAuthority(); }
                catch (Exception ex) { Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug3] AnimateAuthority[{0}] error: {1}", i, ex.Message)); }
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 4: Roam() overhaul — hybrid old/new flow
    //
    // Problem: Current game Roam() has a queue system that hard-returns when
    // the queue is empty, so AIMeE stops dead instead of exploring.
    // Also: AllAimeeQueuedMinables causes multi-AIMeE starvation (first to
    // fill claims ALL ore, others get nothing).
    //
    // Fix: Full Roam() replacement prefix that:
    //  1. Keeps the queue system for efficient multi-target mining
    //  2. NEVER early-returns on empty queue — always drives (like old code)
    //  3. Caps queue to 10 per AIMeE so multiple bots share ore fairly
    //  4. Uses exact game motor/brake formulas (RocketMath.MapToScale)
    //  5. Calls original MovingToMineable() for mining state machine
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug4_RoamFix
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo QueueField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_minableDataQueue");
        private static readonly FieldInfo ReversingField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_reversing");
        private static readonly FieldInfo RoamTimeoutField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_roamTimeout");
        private static readonly FieldInfo DeltaField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "Delta");
        private static readonly FieldInfo ScanTimeoutField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "_minableScanTimeout");
        private static readonly FieldInfo TargetMinableField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "TargetMinable");
        private static readonly FieldInfo SearchAreaField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "MinableSearchArea");
        private static readonly FieldInfo MaxDepthField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "maxMiningDepth");
        private static readonly MethodInfo MovingToMineableMethod =
            AccessTools.Method(typeof(RobotMining), "MovingToMineable");

        // Cap per-AIMeE queue to prevent one bot monopolizing all ore
        private const int MaxQueuePerAimee = 10;

        public static bool Roam_Prefix(RobotMining __instance)
        {
            // Let original handle StorageFull (needs protected InteractMode)
            if (__instance.IsStorageFull)
                return true;

            try
            {
                // --- Queue management ---
                var queue = (List<TargetMinableData>)QueueField.GetValue(__instance);
                int searchArea = (int)SearchAreaField.GetValue(null);
                int maxDepth = (int)MaxDepthField.GetValue(null);

                if (queue.Count <= 0)
                {
                    __instance.ClearMinableQueue();
                    VoxelTerrain.GetAimeeMinableQueue(
                        __instance.ThingTransformPosition,
                        (float)searchArea,
                        queue,
                        maxDepth);

                    // Cap queue: remove excess from front (keep tail = closest)
                    while (queue.Count > MaxQueuePerAimee)
                    {
                        var excess = queue[0];
                        excess.Vein.RemoveAimeeMinableHash(excess.MinableIndex);
                        queue.RemoveAt(0);
                    }
                }

                bool hasTarget = queue.Count > 0;

                // Set target from queue tail (or clear if empty)
                if (hasTarget)
                {
                    TargetMinableField.SetValue(__instance,
                        new TargetMinableData?(queue[queue.Count - 1]));
                }
                // If queue empty: DON'T return — fall through to driving code

                // --- Mining state machine (only when we have ore to mine) ---
                if (hasTarget)
                {
                    bool movingOrMining = (bool)MovingToMineableMethod.Invoke(__instance, null);
                    if (movingOrMining)
                        return false;

                    // Scan timeout countdown
                    if (ScanTimeoutField != null)
                    {
                        float scanTimeout = (float)ScanTimeoutField.GetValue(__instance);
                        if (scanTimeout > 0f)
                        {
                            scanTimeout -= Time.deltaTime;
                            ScanTimeoutField.SetValue(__instance, scanTimeout);
                        }
                    }

                    // Direct mine attempt at current position
                    var targetVal = (TargetMinableData?)TargetMinableField.GetValue(__instance);
                    if (targetVal != null)
                    {
                        Vector3 minePos = targetVal.Value.Vein.GetMinableWorldPosition(
                            targetVal.Value.MinableIndex);
                        Vector3Int minePosInt = new Vector3Int(
                            Mathf.FloorToInt(minePos.x),
                            Mathf.FloorToInt(minePos.y),
                            Mathf.FloorToInt(minePos.z));

                        Ore oreMined;
                        if (targetVal.Value.Vein.TryMineServer(
                            minePosInt, out oreMined, __instance.ThingTransformPosition))
                        {
                            __instance.OnMinedOre(oreMined);
                            targetVal.Value.Vein.RemoveAimeeMinableHash(
                                targetVal.Value.MinableIndex);
                            queue.RemoveAt(queue.Count - 1);
                            TargetMinableField.SetValue(__instance, null);
                            return false; // mined — come back next frame
                        }
                    }
                }

                // --- ALWAYS drive (exact game formulas from Roam) ---
                float power = __instance.Power;
                float velocity = __instance.VelocityMagnitude;
                Vector3 worldCoM = __instance.RigidBody.worldCenterOfMass;

                float reversing = (float)ReversingField.GetValue(__instance);
                if (reversing <= 0f)
                {
                    RaycastHit hit;
                    int mask = ~0;
                    try { mask = CursorManager.Instance.TerrainHitMask; } catch { }
                    if (Physics.Raycast(worldCoM, __instance.ThingTransform.forward,
                        out hit, 0.2f, mask))
                    {
                        reversing = 5f;
                    }
                }

                if (reversing > 0f)
                {
                    __instance.TargetMotorPower = -RocketMath.MapToScale(
                        0f, __instance.MaxSpeed, power, 0f, velocity);
                    reversing -= Time.deltaTime;
                    ReversingField.SetValue(__instance, reversing);
                    return false;
                }
                ReversingField.SetValue(__instance, reversing);

                float roamTimeout = (float)RoamTimeoutField.GetValue(__instance);
                if (roamTimeout <= 0f)
                {
                    roamTimeout = UnityEngine.Random.Range(1f, 3f);
                    float delta = UnityEngine.Random.Range(-15f, 15f);
                    DeltaField.SetValue(__instance, delta);
                    __instance.TargetSteeringAngle = delta;
                }
                roamTimeout -= Time.deltaTime;
                RoamTimeoutField.SetValue(__instance, roamTimeout);

                __instance.TargetMotorPower = RocketMath.MapToScale(
                    0f, __instance.MaxSpeed, power, 0f, velocity);
                __instance.TargetBrakePower = velocity > __instance.MaxSpeed
                    ? RocketMath.MapToScale(
                        __instance.MaxSpeed, __instance.MaxSpeed * 1.3f,
                        0f, __instance.Power, velocity)
                    : 0f;

                // Diagnostics
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog(string.Format("Bug4-Diag-{0}", __instance.GetInstanceID()), 10f))
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo(string.Format(
                        "[Bug4] Roam: queue={0}, globalHash={1}, vel={2:F2}, motor={3:F4}, steer={4:F1}, pos=({5:F0},{6:F0},{7:F0})",
                        queue.Count, Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Count,
                        velocity, __instance.TargetMotorPower, __instance.TargetSteeringAngle,
                        __instance.ThingTransformPosition.x, __instance.ThingTransformPosition.y,
                        __instance.ThingTransformPosition.z));
                }

                return false; // skip original
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug4-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug4] Roam error: {0}", ex.Message));
                return true; // fall through to original on error
            }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 6: MineablesInQueue always 0 on multiplayer clients
    //
    // Root cause: _minableDataQueue is a private local List that is only
    // populated by Roam() on the server (authority). It is NOT a networked
    // property. On clients, it stays empty forever, so GetLogicValue returns 0.
    //
    // Fix: On non-authority clients, when MineablesInQueue is requested,
    // instead of reading the empty local queue, estimate queue using
    // GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface and cap to the roam queue max (10).
    // This is still an estimate (not replicated server queue), but avoids
    // hard-zero and tracks usable queued targets better than raw vicinity.
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug6_QueueClientSync
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo SearchAreaField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "MinableSearchArea");
        private static readonly FieldInfo MaxDepthField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(RobotMining), "maxMiningDepth");
        private const int MaxQueuePerAimee = 10;

        public static void GetLogicValue_Postfix(RobotMining __instance, LogicType logicType, ref double __result)
        {
            // Only act on clients (non-authority) for MineablesInQueue
            if (logicType != LogicType.MineablesInQueue) return;
            if (__instance.HasAuthority) return;

            try
            {
                int searchArea = 32;
                int maxDepth = 3;
                try
                {
                    if (SearchAreaField != null) searchArea = (int)SearchAreaField.GetValue(null);
                    if (MaxDepthField != null) maxDepth = (int)MaxDepthField.GetValue(null);
                }
                catch { }

                int count = VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface(
                    __instance.ThingTransformPosition, (float)searchArea, maxDepth);
                __result = (double)Math.Min(count, MaxQueuePerAimee);
            }
            catch { }
        }
    }

    // =========================================================================
    // BUG 7: MineablesInVicinity overcounts — no per-ore bounds check
    //
    // Root cause: GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface calls GetVeinsInBounds (which
    // uses 32-block chunk lookup), then for each vein calls
    // GetNumberOfReachableMinables — which counts ALL active near-surface ore
    // in the entire vein with NO position filter. Veins can extend well beyond
    // the search area, so ore 50+ blocks away gets counted.
    //
    // Compare to GetAimeeMinableQueue which correctly checks
    // searchBounds.Contains(position) for each individual ore block.
    //
    // Fix: Replace with a bounds-checked version that mirrors GetAimeeMinables
    // logic but only counts instead of building a queue.
    // =========================================================================
    public static class Bug7_VicinityBoundsFix
    {
        private static readonly FieldInfo MinablesField =
            AccessTools.Field(typeof(Vein), "_minables");

        private const float DensityThreshold = 0.49803922f;

        public static bool GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface_Prefix(
            Vector3 position, float boundsSize, int maxDepthFromSurface, ref int __result)
        {
            try
            {
                // Compute search bounds — same math as the private GetVeinsInBounds overload
                float half = boundsSize * 0.5f;
                BoundsInt searchBounds = new BoundsInt(
                    (int)Math.Floor(position.x - half),
                    (int)Math.Floor(position.y - half),
                    (int)Math.Floor(position.z - half),
                    (int)boundsSize + 1,
                    (int)boundsSize + 1,
                    (int)boundsSize + 1);

                // Get veins using the public API
                List<Vein> veins = new List<Vein>();
                Vein.GetVeinsInBounds(searchBounds, ref veins);

                int count = 0;
                int totalMinables = 0;
                int activeCount = 0;
                int inBoundsCount = 0;
                for (int v = 0; v < veins.Count; v++)
                {
                    Vein vein = veins[v];
                    Minable[] minables = (Minable[])MinablesField.GetValue(vein);
                    if (minables == null) continue;

                    totalMinables += minables.Length;
                    Vector3Int veinPos = vein.VeinWorldPosition;
                    for (int i = 0; i < minables.Length; i++)
                    {
                        if (!minables[i].IsActive) continue;
                        activeCount++;

                        Vector3Int worldPos = minables[i].WorldPositionInt(veinPos);

                        // Bounds check — this is what the original is missing
                        if (!searchBounds.Contains(worldPos)) continue;
                        inBoundsCount++;

                        // Near-surface check — same as IsNearSurface
                        if (VoxelTerrain.GetDensityWorldSpace(worldPos + Vector3Int.up * maxDepthFromSurface) < DensityThreshold)
                        {
                            count++;
                        }
                    }
                }

                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug7-Diag", 10f))
                {
                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo(string.Format(
                        "[Bug7] Vicinity diag: veins={0}, totalMinables={1}, active={2}, inBounds={3}, nearSurface={4}, pos=({5:F0},{6:F0},{7:F0}), bounds={8}, depth={9}",
                        veins.Count, totalMinables, activeCount, inBoundsCount, count,
                        position.x, position.y, position.z,
                        boundsSize, maxDepthFromSurface));
                }

                __result = count;
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                // On failure, fall through to original method
                if (Plugin.ShouldLog("Bug7-err", 30f))
                    Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format("[Bug7] Vicinity bounds fix error: {0}", ex.Message));
                return true;
            }
            return false; // skip original
        }
    }
}

```


</details>
</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="copilot_applyPatch">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **copilot_applyPatch** • Generating patch (17 lines) in [](file:///c%3A/Dev/StationpediaAscendedWorkspace/AimeeBugFixes/About/About.xml)</summary>

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ModMetadata xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">
  <Name>AIMeE Bug Fixes</Name>
  <Author>FlorpyDorp</Author>
  <Version>0.4.1</Version>
  <Description>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx to be installed with the StationeersLaunchPad plugin.
See: https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad

Fixes seven bugs with the AIMeE mining robot:

1. Mine Deadlock: AIMeE stops mining after first ore because _mineCancellation is never reset after Mine() completes.
2. Off-State Teleporting: When turned off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports because TryUnstuck/PathToTarget have no OnOff guard.
3. Tread Animation Desync: On dedicated server clients, tread UV animations are frozen because WheelRpm is never set on non-authority clients.
4. Roam Regression: AIMeE stops exploring when no ore is in scan range because driving logic is skipped when queue is empty. Also restores MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32.
5. Pickup Launch: Picking up/dragging AIMeE launches the player because wheel motor torque transfers through the drag joint.
6. MineablesInQueue Client Sync: MineablesInQueue always reads 0 on multiplayer clients because the queue is a local server-only list.
7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount: Vicinity count includes ore from entire veins beyond the search area due to missing per-ore bounds check.

Works on both dedicated servers and clients.</Description>

  <ChangeLog>
v0.4.1: MineablesInQueue client fallback restored
- Re-enable Bug 6 patch for MineablesInQueue on non-authority clients
- Return capped queue estimate (max 10) instead of hard-zero on clients
- Keep dedicated-server authority behavior unchanged (real queue still server-side)

v0.4.0: Full Roam() overhaul + multi-AIMeE fairness
- Replace Bug 4 postfix with full Roam() prefix replacement
- Hybrid old/new flow: queue system + always-drive (never stops on empty queue)
- Cap queue to 10 per AIMeE to prevent AllAimeeQueuedMinables starvation
- Use exact game motor/brake formulas (RocketMath.MapToScale)
- Call original MovingToMineable() for mining state machine
- Mine at position using game's exact TryMineServer flow with queue dequeue

v0.3.0: Bug 7 enabled, Bug 6 removed, roam fix
- Enable Bug 7 MineablesInVicinity bounds-check (was incorrectly disabled)
- Remove Bug 6 fake queue proxy that made MineablesInQueue == MineablesInVicinity
- Improve Bug 4 roam exploration: longer steering persistence (4-8s) to reduce circling
- Keep MinableSearchArea restored to 32 (original value when AIMeE worked well)

v0.2.3: Bug 7 fix + Bug 6 ambiguous method fix
- Fix MineablesInVicinity overcounting ore outside search bounds (Bug 7)
- Fix GetLogicValue ambiguous match error from two-overload resolution (Bug 6)

v0.2.2: Bug 6 fix
- Fix MineablesInQueue always 0 on multiplayer clients (Bug 6)

v0.2.1: Bug 5 fix
- Fix picking up AIMeE launching player into the air (Bug 5)
- Changed UpdateEachFrame from prefix to postfix to preserve view-following

v0.2.0: Bugs 3 and 4
- Fix tread UV animation on multiplayer clients via WheelRpm estimation from velocity (Bug 3)
- Fix server-side AnimateAuthority skipped when occluded (Bug 3)
- Fix Roam regression: restore driving logic when ore queue is empty (Bug 4)
- Restore MinableSearchArea from 16 to 32 (Bug 4)

v0.1.0: Initial release
- Fix mine deadlock: _mineCancellation reset after Mine completes (Bug 1)
- Fix off-state teleporting: skip TryUnstuck/PathToTarget when off (Bug 2)
  </ChangeLog>
  <Tags>
    <Tag>StationeersLaunchPad</Tag>
    <Tag>BugFix</Tag>
    <Tag>Server</Tag>
  </Tags>
  <InGameDescription><![CDATA[<size="30"><color=#ffa500>AIMeE Bug Fixes</color></size>
<color=#ff0000>This is a Plugin Mod. It requires BepInEx with StationeersLaunchPad.</color>

Fixes seven AIMeE mining robot bugs:

<color=#ffa500>1. Mine Deadlock</color>
AIMeE stops after first ore because the mining cancellation token is never reset.

<color=#ffa500>2. Off-State Teleporting</color>
When off but receiving logic, AIMeE hops/teleports due to missing power guards.

<color=#ffa500>3. Tread Animation Desync</color>
Tread animations frozen on multiplayer clients. WheelRpm estimated from synced velocity.

<color=#ffa500>4. Roam Regression</color>
AIMeE stops exploring when no ore nearby. Driving logic restored when queue empty. Scan range restored to 32.

<color=#ffa500>5. Pickup Launch</color>
Picking up AIMeE launches the player. Motor/brakes zeroed while dragged.

<color=#ffa500>6. MineablesInQueue (Multiplayer)</color>
Always 0 on clients. Uses terrain query as fallback for non-authority.

<color=#ffa500>7. MineablesInVicinity Overcount</color>
Counts ore beyond search area. Added per-ore bounds check matching queue logic.
]]></InGameDescription>
</ModMetadata>

```


</details>
</tool-use>

Next I’ll build once to verify the patch compiles cleanly.**Preparing deployment packaging command****Handling locked DLLs and updating changelog**Build succeeded with the queue fix, but copying into your mods folder failed because the DLL is currently locked by a running process.
