Color.black;\n\n                _panelStyle = new GUIStyle(_style);\n                _panelStyle.fontSize = 13;\n                _panelStyle.alignment = TextAnchor.UpperLeft;\n                _panelStyle.normal.textColor = Color.white;\n\n                _bgStyle = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.box);\n                _bgStyle.normal.background = MakeTexture(1, 1, new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.7f));\n cam.WorldToScreenPoint(kvp.Value.WorldPos);\n                if (screenPos.z &lt;= 0f || screenPos.z &gt; 100f) continue;\n\n                float x = screenPos.x;\n                float y = Screen.height - screenPos.y;\n                float scale = Mathf.Clamp(1f - (screenPos.z - 5f) / 60f, 0.5f, 1.5f);\n                int fontSize = Mathf.RoundToInt(14 * scale);\n                _style.fontSize = fontSize;\n                _shadowStyle.fontSize = fontSize;\n\n                float width = 350f * scale;\n                float height = 200f * scale;\n                Rect rect = new Rect(x - width * 0.5f, y - height, width, height);\n                Rect shadowRect = new Rect(rect.x + 1, rect.y + 1, rect.width, rect.height);\n\n                GUI.Label(shadowRect, kvp.Value.OverlayText, _shadowStyle);\n                GUI.Label(rect, kvp.Value.OverlayText, _style);\n            }\n\n            // Global panel\n            Rect bgRect = new Rect(10, 10, 260, 75);\n            GUI.Box(bgRect, \"\", _bgStyle);\n            GUI.Label(new Rect(15, 12, 250, 70), _globalPanelText, _panelStyle);\n        }\n\n        private static void DetectStateChanges(RobotMining aimee, int id,\n            int queueCount, bool hasTarget, bool isMining)\n        {\n            AimeeState current = new AimeeState\n            {\n (prev.IsMining != current.IsMining)\n                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo(string.Format(\"[Debug] AIMeE#{0} Mining: {1} -&gt; {2}\", id, prev.IsMining, current.IsMining));\n                if (prev.HasTarget != current.HasTarget)\n                    Plugin.Log.LogInfo(string.Format(\"[Debug] AIMeE#{0} HasTarget: {1} -&gt; {2}\", id, prev.HasTarget, current.HasTarget));\n                if   "textEditContent": "        private static GUIStyle _panelStyle;\n        private static GUIStyle _bgStyle;\n\n        // Cached data updated at low frequency from Update()\n        private static float _lastCacheTime;\n        private const float CacheInterval = 0.5f;\n        private static RobotMining[] _cachedAimees = new RobotMining[0];\n        private static readonly Dictionary&lt;int, CachedAimeeData&gt; _cachedData =\n            new Dictionary&lt;int, CachedAimeeData&gt;();\n        private static string _globalPanelText = \"\";\n\n        // Per-AIMeE diagnostic data from last queue fill\n        private static readonly Dictionary&lt;int, QueueFillDiag&gt; _lastQueueFill =\n            new Dictionary&lt;int, QueueFillDiag&gt;();\n\n        // Per-AIMeE last known state for change detection\n        private static readonly Dictionary&lt;int, AimeeState&gt; _lastState =\n            new Dictionary&lt;int, AimeeState&gt;();\n\n        internal struct QueueFillDiag\n        {\n            public int HashBefore;\n            public int HashAfterClear;\n            public int RawQueueCount;\n            public int HashAfterFill;\n            public int CappedQueueCount;\n            public float Timestamp;\n        }\n\n        internal struct AimeeState\n        {\n            public int Mode;\n            public int QueueCount;\n            public bool IsStorageFull;\n            public bool IsMining;\n            public bool HasTarget;\n        }\n\n        internal struct CachedAimeeData\n        {\n            public string OverlayText;\n            public Vector3 WorldPos;\n        }\n\n        public static string HandleCommand(string[] args)\n        {\n            if (args.Length &gt; 0)\n            {\n                if (args[0].ToLower() == \"on\") Enabled = true;\n                else if (args[0].ToLower() == \"off\") Enabled = false;\n                else return \"Usage: aimeedebug [on|off]\";\n            }\n            else\n            {\n                Enabled = !Enabled;\n            }\n            string state = Enabled ? \"ENABLED\" : \"DISABLED\";\n            Plugin.Log.LogInfo(string.Format(\"[Debug] AIMeE debug overlay {0}\", state));\n            return string.Format(\"AIMeE debug overlay: {0}\", state);\n        }\n\n        public static void UpdateQueueFillData(int instanceId, int hashBefore,\n            int hashAfterClear, int rawQueue, int hashAfterFill, int cappedQueue)\n        {\n            _lastQueueFill[instanceId] = new QueueFillDiag\n            {\n                HashBefore = hashBefore,\n                HashAfterClear = hashAfterClear,\n                RawQueueCount = rawQueue,\n                HashAfterFill = hashAfterFill,\n                CappedQueueCount = cappedQueue,\n                Timestamp = Time.time\n            };\n        }\n\n        /// &lt;summary&gt;\n        /// Called from Plugin.Update() — does all expensive work at low frequency.\n        /// &lt;/summary&gt;\n        public static void UpdateCache()\n        {\n            float now = Time.time;\n            if (now - _lastCacheTime &lt; CacheInterval) return;\n            _lastCacheTime = now;\n\n            _cachedAimees = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType&lt;RobotMining&gt;();\n            _cachedData.Clear();\n\n            int searchArea = 32;\n            int maxDepth = 3;\n            try\n            {\n                if (Bug4_RoamFix.SearchAreaField != null) searchArea = (int)Bug4_RoamFix.SearchAreaField.GetValue(null);\n                if (Bug4_RoamFix.MaxDepthField != null) maxDepth = (int)Bug4_RoamFix.MaxDepthField.GetValue(null);\n            }\n            catch { }\n\n            foreach (var aimee in _cachedAimees)\n            {\n                try\n                {\n                    int id = aimee.GetInstanceID();\n                    var queue = (List&lt;TargetMinableData&gt;)Bug4_RoamFix.QueueField.GetValue(aimee);\n                    var targetRaw = Bug4_RoamFix.TargetMinableField.GetValue(aimee);\n                    var cancelWrapper = (CancellationTokenWrapper)Bug4_RoamFix.MineCancellationField.GetValue(aimee);\n\n                    bool hasTarget = targetRaw != null &amp;&amp; ((TargetMinableData?)targetRaw) != null;\n                    bool isMining = cancelWrapper != null &amp;&amp; cancelWrapper.Initialized;\n                    int queueCount = queue != null ? queue.Count : -1;\n\n                    int vicinity = VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface(\n                        aimee.ThingTransformPosition, (float)searchArea, maxDepth);\n\n                    string targetInfo = \"none\";\n                    if (hasTarget)\n                    {\n                        var target = ((TargetMinableData?)targetRaw).Value;\n                        Vector3 tPos = target.Vein.GetMinableWorldPosition(target.MinableIndex);\n                        float tDist = Vector3.Distance(aimee.ThingTransformPosition, tPos);\n                        bool tActive = target.Vein.GetActive(target.MinableIndex);\n                        targetInfo = string.Format(\"d={0:F1} {1}\", tDist, tActive ? \"ACTIVE\" : \"DEAD\");\n                    }\n\n                    string fillInfo = \"no data\";\n                    if (_lastQueueFill.TryGetValue(id, out QueueFillDiag diag))\n                    {\n                        float age = now - diag.Timestamp;\n                        fillInfo = string.Format(\"raw={0} hPre={1} hClr={2} hPost={3} ({4:F0}s ago)\",\n                            diag.RawQueueCount, diag.HashBefore, diag.HashAfterClear,\n                            diag.HashAfterFill, age);\n                    }\n\n                    string text = string.Format(\n                        \"=== AIMeE #{0} ===\\n\" +\n                        \"Mode: {1}  On: {2}  Power: {3}\\n\" +\n                        \"Queue: {4}  Vicinity: {5}\\n\" +\n                        \"GlobalHash: {6}\\n\" +\n                        \"Target: {7}\\n\" +\n                        \"Mining: {8}  StorageFull: {9}\\n\" +\n                        \"Vel: {10:F1}  Motor: {11:F3}\\n\" +\n                        \"LastFill: {12}\",\n                        id, aimee.Mode, aimee.OnOff, aimee.Powered,\n                        queueCount, vicinity,\n                        Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Count,\n                        targetInfo,\n                        isMining, aimee.IsStorageFull,\n                        aimee.VelocityMagnitude, aimee.TargetMotorPower,\n                        fillInfo);\n\n                    _cachedData[id] = new CachedAimeeData\n                    {\n                        OverlayText = text,\n                        WorldPos = aimee.ThingTransformPosition + Vector3.up * 2.5f\n                    };\n\n                    // Detect state changes\n                    DetectStateChanges(aimee, id, queueCount, hasTarget, isMining);\n                }\n                catch (Exception ex)\n                {\n                    if (Plugin.ShouldLog(\"Debug-Cache-err\", 10f))\n                        Plugin.Log.LogError(string.Format(\"[Debug] Cache update error: {0}\", ex.Message));\n                }\n            }\n\n            _globalPanelText = string.Format(\n                \"=== AIMeE Debug ===\\n\" +\n                \"AIMeEs found: {0}\\n\" +\n                \"AllAimeeQueuedMinables: {1}\\n\" +\n                \"Time: {2:F1}\",\n                _cachedAimees.Length,\n                Vein.AllAimeeQueuedMinables.Count,\n                now);\n        }\n\n        /// _style.fontSize = fontSize;\n                _shadowStyle.fontSize = fontSize;\n\n                float width = 350f * scale;\n                float height = 200f * scale;\n                Rect rect = new Rect(x - width * 0.5f, y - height, width, height);\n                Rect shadowRect = new Rect(rect.x + 1, rect.y + 1, rect.width, rect.height);\n\n                GUI.Label(shadowRect, kvp.Value.OverlayText, _shadowStyle);\n                GUI.Label(rect, kvp.Value.OverlayText, _style);\n            }\n\n            // Global panel\n            Rect bgRect = new Rect(10, 10, 260, 75);\n            GUI.Box(bgRect, \"\", _bgStyle);\n            GUI.Label(new Rect(15, 12, 250, 70), _globalPanelText, _panelStyle);\n        }\n\n        private static void ---



Two requests:
1. When AIMeEs are in mode 3, log MineablesInVicinity and MineablesInQueue every 0.5s
2. Fix the FPS drop from the debug overlay

The FPS drop is almost certainly from `FindObjectsOfType<RobotMining>()` being called every OnGUI frame (which fires multiple times per frame), and also from calling `VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface` for every AIMeE every OnGUI call. Let me fix both issues.

For the mode 3 logging - I can add that to the Roam_Prefix directly since that's already called every frame when in mode 3. Just log vicinity and queue count every 0.5s.


For the FPS fix, I need to cache the FindObjectsOfType result and only refresh it every 0.5s in Update rather than every OnGUI call, and remove the expensive terrain query from the overlay entirely by using the vicinity value that's already being computed in the Bug7 diagnostics. Let me check the current overlay code to see what I'm working with. Now let me see the Bug4 Roam prefix to add Mode 3 logging there: Now I have the full picture. The FPS drop is from two things happening every OnGUI call (multiple times per frame):
1. `FindObjectsOfType<RobotMining>()` — expensive scene search
2. `VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface()` per AIMeE — expensive terrain query

I'll cache the data in `Update()` and make OnGUI just render cached data, plus add the mode 3 periodic logging. Now I'll make all the changes:

1. Move expensive work (FindObjectsOfType, terrain queries, state detection) into `UpdateCache()` called from `Plugin.Update()` at 2Hz
2. Make OnGUI only render cached strings
3. Add mode 3 vicinity/queue logging every 0.5s in the Roam prefix Done. Two changes:

**Mode 3 logging every 0.5s** — whenever an AIMeE is in roam (mode 3), it now logs:
```
[Mode3] AIMeE#-542182: vicinity=4, queue=7, globalHash=7, vel=2.31, motor=0.0012, pos=(-595,127,803)
```

**FPS fix** — the overlay was killing performance because `FindObjectsOfType` + terrain vicinity queries were running inside `OnGUI()` (called multiple times per frame). Now all expensive work happens in `Update()` at 2Hz (every 0.5s), and `OnGUI()` just renders cached strings.

The user wants several things:

1. Add per-AIMeE vicinity diagnostics every 1 second
2. Add vicinity diag info to the hover overlay in a different color
3. Add more logging to understand why AIMeEs switch to mode 2 (MoveToTarget/return home) unexpectedly
4. The user's IC script sends AIMeEs home when MineablesInVicinity == 0, but they're going home early

The key mystery: AIMeEs switch from mode 3 to mode 2 even with ore nearby. The user's IC reads MineablesInVicinity and if it's 0, sets mode to 2. 

But the logs show vicinity > 0 when they switch modes. This could be a timing issue where the IC reads a 0 value at the exact moment it checks, even though ore appears moments later, or there's a mismatch between what the client and server see. I need to add logging that captures MineablesInVicinity and MineablesInQueue values at the exact moment a mode change happens, so we can see if the IC is legitimately reading 0 when the switch occurs.

I should also intercept the GetLogicValue call for MineablesInVicinity to log what value the IC chip actually receives when it reads this property. If the Bug7 prefix is throwing an exception and falling through to the original code, that would explain unexpected behavior—though the original code overcounts rather than undercounts, so that doesn't quite fit. Looking at the logs more carefully, the nearSurface count varies wildly based on position: at some coordinates it's 0, at others it's 4 or 6. As the AIMeE moves around, MineablesInVicinity fluctuates between 0 and small numbers, and the IC is sending them home when it hits those 0 positions.

The key is to patch GetLogicValue to log whenever the IC reads MineablesInVicinity, especially when it returns 0, and make the Bug7 diagnostics per-AIMeE with a 1-second interval instead of using a global throttle. Let me trace through how GetLogicValue retrieves the MineablesInVicinity value in RobotMining to see if it's bypassing our Bug7 diagnostic.Let me first understand how the game returns MineablesInVicinity to the IC chip — that's what the script reads to decide whether to go home. Now I can see the critical path. When the IC reads `MineablesInVicinity`, it calls `GetLogicValue` which calls `VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface` — which our Bug7 prefix intercepts. The IC is reading this value EVERY tick and sometimes seeing 0.

Now let me check how Mode gets set via IC: Let me also check how Mode gets set externally (via IC), and read the current Plugin.cs:

```csharp
if (logicType == LogicType.MineablesInVicinity)

        base.ThingTransformPosition, (float)RobotMining.MinableSearchArea, RobotMining.maxMiningDepth);
}
```


This calls `VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface` → **intercepted by Bug7 prefix** → returns bounds-checked, near-surface count.

Writing the diagnostic implementation...You've asked an excellent question. Let me trace both code paths precisely:

**Path 1 — Our debug overlay** (in `UpdateCache`, `undefined`):
```csharp
int vicinity = VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface(
    aimee.ThingTransformPosition, (float)searchArea, maxDepth);
```

**Path 2 — The IC chip reads `MineablesInVicinity`** (game's `RobotMining.GetLogicValue`):
```csharp
return (double)VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface(
    base.ThingTransformPosition, (float)RobotMining.MinableSearchArea, RobotMining.maxMiningDepth);
```

**They both call the exact same function** (`VoxelTerrain.GetNumberOfMinablesNearSurface`) with the same parameters, and **Bug7's prefix intercepts both calls identically**. There is no separate code path — the IC reads through our Bug7 fix.

The difference you're seeing is almost certainly **timing**: our overlay caches at 2Hz (every 0.5 seconds), while the IC reads in real-time every tick. Since the AIMeE is moving, the vicinity count can swing dramatically between positions (e.g., moving 2 blocks closer to exposed ore goes from 0 → 15).

**But here's what matters for the premature return:** Bug7's accurate count CAN return 0 when the AIMeE is at a position where all nearby ore is deeper than 3 blocks. The original game code would have overcounted (no bounds check), returning like 40+. So your IC script sees `MineablesInVicinity == 0` at certain positions that the old code would have reported as non-zero — triggering your "go home" logic.
